9. 
10. > ÔÜá´ŞĆ **The log directory must already exist.** The shim will not create it. If it doesn't exist, no logs will be written and there will be no error or indication of failure.
11. 
12. Logs are written by mscoreei.dll (the shim implementation DLL loaded by mscoree.dll). They are written while the process runs and **cannot be opened until the process exits**.
13. 
14. ## File Naming
15. 
16. ```
17. {ProcessName}.CLRLoad{NN}.log
18. ```
19. 
20. - `{ProcessName}` = the EXE name (e.g., `mt.exe`, `csc.exe`, `MSBuild.exe`)
21. - `{NN}` = sequence number 00ÔÇô99 (increments if the previous file exists, one per process instance)
22. - Multiple log files for the same EXE indicate multiple process invocations
23. 
24. ## Line Format
25. 
26. Every line in the log follows this format:
27. 
28. ```
29. ThreadID,TickCount.Milliseconds,Message
30. ```
31. 
32. - **ThreadID**: OS thread ID (decimal) that produced the log entry
33. - **TickCount.Milliseconds**: Time in `GetTickCount()` units ÔÇö seconds since system boot, with millisecond precision. Useful for ordering events and measuring elapsed time within and across logs.
34. - **Message**: The log message (see below)
35. 
36. ### Header Lines
37. 
38. The first three lines of every log are:
39. 
40. ```
41. {tid},{tick},CLR Loading log for {full_path_to_exe}
42. {tid},{tick},Log started at {time} on {date}
43. {tid},{tick},-----------------------------------
44. ```
45. 
46. The process path in the first line identifies exactly which binary triggered CLR activation.
47. 
48. ## Log Message Reference
49. 
50. ### Entry Points
51. 
52. | Message | Meaning |
53. |---------|---------|
54. | `FunctionCall: _CorExeMain` | Managed EXE launch ÔÇö the OS loader recognized a .NET assembly |
55. | `FunctionCall: DllGetClassObject. Clsid: {guid}, Iid: {iid}` | COM activation ÔÇö CoCreateInstance routed through mscoree.dll |
56. | `FunctionCall: ClrCreateInstance, Clsid: {guid}, Iid: {iid}` | Modern v4+ hosting API entry |
57. | `LegacyFunctionCall: CorBindToRuntimeEx. Version: {ver}, BuildFlavor: {flavor}, Flags: {hex}` | Legacy v1/v2 hosting API binding |
58. | `LegacyFunctionCall: LoadLibraryShim. DllName: {dll}, Version: {ver}` | Legacy API to load a framework DLL |
59. | `LegacyFunctionCall: GetFileVersion. Filename: {path}` | Shim reading PE version from a binary |
60. | `MethodCall: ICLRMetaHostPolicy::GetRequestedRuntime. Version: {ver}, Metahost Policy Flags: {hex}, Binary: {path}` | Policy-based runtime request |
61. | `MethodCall: ICLRRuntimeInfo::GetInterface. Clsid: {guid}, Iid: {iid}` | Requesting an interface from a loaded runtime |
62. 
63. ### Version Computation
64. 
65. | Message | Meaning |
66. |---------|---------|
67. | `Input values for ComputeVersionString follow this line` | Start of a version resolution block |
68. | `IsLegacyBind is: {0\|1}` | Whether this is a legacy (pre-v4) activation path |
69. | `IsCapped is {0\|1}` | Whether enumeration is restricted to ÔëĄv2.0.50727 |
70. | `SkuCheckFlags are {value}` | SKU compatibility check mode |
71. | `ShouldEmulateExeLaunch is {0\|1}` | Whether to use EXE launch policies |
72. | `LegacyBindRequired is {0\|1}` | Whether legacy binding is strictly required |
73. | `Installed Runtime: vX.Y.Z. VERSION_ARCHITECTURE: N` | A runtime version found installed on the machine |
74. 
75. ### Config File Processing
76. 
77. | Message | Meaning |
78. |---------|---------|
79. | `Parsing config file: {path}` | Looking for an application config file |
80. | `Config File (Open). Result:00000000` | Config file found and opened successfully |
81. | `Config File (Open). Result:80070002` | Config file **not found** (ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND) |
82. | `Config File (Read). Result:00000000` | Config file read successfully |
83. | `Found config file: {path}` | Config file successfully parsed |
84. | `UseLegacyV2RuntimeActivationPolicy is set to {0\|1}` | Value of `<startup useLegacyV2RuntimeActivationPolicy="true\|false">` |
85. | `Config file includes SupportedRuntime entry. Version: {ver}, SKU: {sku}` | A `<supportedRuntime>` element from the config |
86. | `Found a supportedRuntime tag in the config file` | At least one `<supportedRuntime>` was present |
87. 
88. ### Runtime Selection Outcomes
89. 
90. | Message | Meaning |
91. |---------|---------|
92. | `Using supportedRuntime: vX.Y.Z` | Shim selected this version from config's `<supportedRuntime>` list |
93. | `{exe} was built with version: vX.Y.Z` | PE header version from a managed binary |
94. | `FindLatestVersion is returning the following version: vX.Y.Z Input VERSION_ARCHITECTURE: N, V2.0 Capped: {0\|1}` | Result of policy-based latest-version search |
95. | `Default version of the runtime on the machine: vX.Y.Z` | Resolved default version |
96. | `Default version of the runtime on the machine: (null)` | **No runtime found** ÔÇö resolution failed |
97. | `Decided on runtime: vX.Y.Z` | **Final decision** ÔÇö this version will be loaded |
98. | `Runtime has been loaded. Version: vX.Y.Z` | CLR successfully loaded into the process |
99. | `V2.0 Capping is preventing consideration of a newer runtime` | A v4+ runtime was skipped because capping is active |
100. 
101. ### Errors and FOD
102. 
103. | Message | Meaning |
104. |---------|---------|
105. | `ERROR: Unable to find a version of the runtime to use.` | Version resolution failed completely |
106. | `ERROR: Version vX.Y.Z is not present on the machine.` | A specific requested version is not installed |
107. | `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to {value}` | Process error mode check before showing dialogs. 0 = dialogs allowed. Nonzero = dialogs suppressed. |
108. | `Checking if feature-on-demand installation would help` | Shim is re-running version computation to check if installing .NET 3.5 would help |
109. | `Launching feature-on-demand installation. CmdLine: {cmd}` | **FOD dialog is being shown** ÔÇö fondue.exe is launched |
110. | `Could have launched feature-on-demand installation if was not opted out. CmdLine: {cmd}` | FOD was suppressed because SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set |
111. 
112. ### Process Lifecycle
113. 
114. | Message | Meaning |
115. |---------|---------|
116. | `FunctionCall: OnShimDllMainCalled. Reason: {code}` | DllMain callback (1=PROCESS_ATTACH, 0=PROCESS_DETACH, 2=THREAD_ATTACH, 3=THREAD_DETACH) |
117. | `FunctionCall: RealDllMain. Reason: {code}` | Actual DllMain processing |
118. | `LegacyFunctionCall: CorExitProcess. Code: {exit_code}` | Process exiting through legacy API |
119. 
120. ### Runtime Info Queries
121. 
122. | Message | Meaning |
123. |---------|---------|
124. | `MethodCall: ICLRRuntimeInfo::GetVersionString. Version: vX.Y.Z` | Querying runtime version |
125. | `MethodCall: ICLRRuntimeInfo::GetRuntimeDirectory. Version: vX.Y.Z` | Querying runtime install path |
126. | `MethodCall: ICLRRuntimeInfo::LoadLibrary. Name: {dll}. Version: vX.Y.Z` | Loading a framework DLL |

128. ## Well-Known CLSIDs
129. 
130. These CLSIDs frequently appear in COM activation logs:
131. 
132. | CLSID | Name | Notes |
133. |-------|------|-------|
134. | `{E5CB7A31-7512-11D2-89CE-0080C792E5D8}` | CorSymWriter_SxS / CLR Meta Data | Debug symbol writer ÔÇö common trigger for legacy COM activation in native build tools |
135. | `{0A29FF9E-7F9C-4437-8B11-F424491E3931}` | NDP SymBinder | Debug symbol binder |
136. | `{9280188D-0E8E-4867-B30C-7FA83884E8DE}` | CLRMetaHost | ICLRMetaHost ÔÇö the v4+ entry point for hosting |

139. | `{F7721072-BF57-476D-89F8-A7625D27683A}` | CLRStrongName | Strong name APIs |
140. | `{B79B0ACD-F5CD-409B-B5A5-A16244610B92}` | ALink | Assembly linker |
141. 
142. ## Well-Known IIDs
143. 
144. | IID | Interface |
145. |-----|-----------|
146. | `{00000001-0000-0000-C000-000000000046}` | IClassFactory |
147. | `{D332DB9E-B9B3-4125-8207-A14884F53216}` | ICLRMetaHost |
148. | `{E2190695-77B2-492E-8E14-C4B3A7FDD593}` | ICLRMetaHostPolicy |
149. | `{BD39D1D2-BA2F-486A-89B0-B4B0CB466891}` | ICLRRuntimeInfo |

152. | `{07C4E752-3CBA-4A07-9943-B5F206382178}` | ICLRRuntimeHost |
153. 
154. ## SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS Values
155. 
156. The `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to {value}` line reports the result of `SetErrorMode(0)` (which returns the previous mode). Common values:
157. 
158. | Value | Hex | Flags Set | FOD Allowed? |
159. |-------|-----|-----------|-------------|
160. | 0 | 0x0000 | None | **Yes** |
161. | 1 | 0x0001 | SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS | No |
162. | 32769 | 0x8001 | SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS + SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX | No |
163. | 32768 | 0x8000 | SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX only | **Yes** (only SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS bit matters) |
164. 
165. Any nonzero value where bit 0 (SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS = 0x0001) is set will suppress the FOD dialog.
166. 
167. ## Nested / Re-Entrant Log Entries
168. 
169. Log entries within a single activation sequence can include the shim's own internal calls into its own APIs. This creates nested blocks that can be confusing if you don't expect them:
170. 
171. - A `DllGetClassObject` call internally triggers `ComputeVersionString`, which may call `FindLatestVersion` ÔÇö all generating log lines within the same sequence
172. - When the FOD check runs ("Checking if feature-on-demand installation would help"), it **re-runs the entire version computation** ÔÇö producing a second `Input values for ComputeVersionString` block. This is the shim asking "if .NET 3.5 were installed, would that resolve this request?"
173. - A `GetRequestedRuntime` call may internally trigger `GetFileVersion` and config parsing
174. 
175. The key to reading these is: watch for the major entry point lines (`FunctionCall:`, `MethodCall:`) to distinguish top-level activations from internal re-entrant calls.
176. 
177. ## .NET 3.5 / v2.0.50727 Version Mapping
178. 
179. In the logs, all version strings refer to the **CLR runtime version**, not the .NET Framework marketing version:
180. 
181. | Runtime Version String | CLR Version | .NET Framework Versions |
182. |----------------------|-------------|------------------------|
183. | `v1.0.3705` | CLR 1.0 | .NET Framework 1.0 |
184. | `v1.1.4322` | CLR 1.1 | .NET Framework 1.1 |
185. | `v2.0.50727` | CLR 2.0 | .NET Framework 2.0, 3.0, 3.5 |
186. | `v4.0.30319` | CLR 4.0 | .NET Framework 4.0 through 4.8.x |
187. 
188. .NET 2.0, 3.0, and 3.5 all share CLR v2.0 ÔÇö the "3.0" and "3.5" releases added libraries on top of the same runtime. When the shim resolves to `v2.0.50727` or FOD offers to install "NetFx3", it's all about CLR v2.0.
189. 
190. ## Shim HRESULT Codes
191. 
192. These HRESULTs are returned by the shim to callers. They don't appear in the activation logs themselves (which use human-readable messages), but knowing them helps connect caller-side errors to activation log analysis:
193. 
194. | HRESULT | Symbol | Meaning |
195. |---------|--------|---------|
196. | `0x80131700` | `CLR_E_SHIM_RUNTIMELOAD` | Cannot find or load a suitable runtime. The most common shim error ÔÇö corresponds to "ERROR: Unable to find a version of the runtime to use" in logs. |
197. | `0x80131701` | `CLR_E_SHIM_RUNTIMEEXPORT` | Found a runtime but failed to get a required export or interface. |
198. | `0x80131702` | `CLR_E_SHIM_INSTALLROOT` | .NET Framework install root missing or invalid in registry. |
199. | `0x80131703` | `CLR_E_SHIM_INSTALLCOMP` | A required installation component is missing. |
200. | `0x80131704` | `CLR_E_SHIM_LEGACYRUNTIMEALREADYBOUND` | A different runtime is already bound as the legacy runtime ÔÇö a legacy API tried to bind to a conflicting version. |
201. | `0x80131705` | `CLR_E_SHIM_SHUTDOWNINPROGRESS` | The shim is shutting down. |
202.

 ### Runtime Selection Outcomes
 
 | Message | Meaning |
 |---------|---------|
 | `Using supportedRuntime: vX.Y.Z` | Shim selected this version from config's `<supportedRuntime>` list |
 | `{exe} was built with version: vX.Y.Z` | PE header version from a managed binary |

 | `Default version of the runtime on the machine: (null)` | **No runtime found** ÔÇö resolution failed |

 | `Runtime has been loaded. Version: vX.Y.Z` | CLR successfully loaded into the process |
 | `V2.0 Capping is preventing consideration of a newer runtime` | A v4+ runtime was skipped because capping is active |
 
 ### Errors and FOD
 
 | Message | Meaning |
 |---------|---------|
 | `ERROR: Unable to find a version of the runtime to use.` | Version resolution failed completely |
 | `ERROR: Version vX.Y.Z is not present on the machine.` | A specific requested version is not installed |
 | `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to {value}` | Process error mode check before showing dialogs. 0 = dialogs allowed. Nonzero = dialogs suppressed. |
 | `Checking if feature-on-demand installation would help` | Shim is re-running version computation to check if installing .NET 3.5 would help |
 | `Launching feature-on-demand installation. CmdLine: {cmd}` | **FOD dialog is being shown** ÔÇö fondue.exe is launched |
 | `Could have launched feature-on-demand installation if was not opted out. CmdLine: {cmd}` | FOD was suppressed because SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set |
 
 ### Process Lifecycle
 
 | Message | Meaning |
 |---------|---------|
 | `FunctionCall: OnShimDllMainCalled. Reason: {code}` | DllMain callback (1=PROCESS_ATTACH, 0=PROCESS_DETACH, 2=THREAD_ATTACH, 3=THREAD_DETACH) |
 | `FunctionCall: RealDllMain. Reason: {code}` | Actual DllMain processing |
 | `LegacyFunctionCall: CorExitProcess. Code: {exit_code}` | Process exiting through legacy API |

 | Message | Meaning |
 |---------|---------|
 | `MethodCall: ICLRRuntimeInfo::GetVersionString. Version: vX.Y.Z` | Querying runtime version |

 ## Well-Known CLSIDs
 
 These CLSIDs frequently appear in COM activation logs:

 | `{CB2F6723-AB3A-11D2-9C40-00C04FA30A3E}` | CorRuntimeHost | Legacy v1/v2 hosting |
 | `{F7721072-BF57-476D-89F8-A7625D27683A}` | CLRStrongName | Strong name APIs |
 | `{B79B0ACD-F5CD-409B-B5A5-A16244610B92}` | ALink | Assembly linker |

 | IID | Interface |
 |-----|-----------|
 | `{00000001-0000-0000-C000-000000000046}` | IClassFactory |
 | `{D332DB9E-B9B3-4125-8207-A14884F53216}` | ICLRMetaHost |
 | `{E2190695-77B2-492E-8E14-C4B3A7FDD593}` | ICLRMetaHostPolicy |
 | `{BD39D1D2-BA2F-486A-89B0-B4B0CB466891}` | ICLRRuntimeInfo |
 | `{31BCFCE2-DAFB-11D2-9F81-00C04F79A0A3}` | IMetaDataDispenserEx |
 | `{CB2F6722-AB3A-11D2-9C40-00C04FA30A3E}` | ICorRuntimeHost |
 | `{07C4E752-3CBA-4A07-9943-B5F206382178}` | ICLRRuntimeHost |
 
 ## SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS Values
 
 The `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS is set to {value}` line reports the result of `SetErrorMode(0)` (which returns the previous mode). Common values:
 
 | Value | Hex | Flags Set | FOD Allowed? |
 |-------|-----|-----------|-------------|
 | 0 | 0x0000 | None | **Yes** |
 | 1 | 0x0001 | SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS | No |
 | 32769 | 0x8001 | SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS + SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX | No |
 | 32768 | 0x8000 | SEM_NOGPFAULTERRORBOX only | **Yes** (only SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS bit matters) |
 
 Any nonzero value where bit 0 (SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS = 0x0001) is set will suppress the FOD dialog.
 
 ## Nested / Re-Entrant Log Entries
 
 Log entries within a single activation sequence can include the shim's own internal calls into its own APIs. This creates nested blocks that can be confusing if you don't expect them:
 
 - A `DllGetClassObject` call internally triggers `ComputeVersionString`, which may call `FindLatestVersion` ÔÇö all generating log lines within the same sequence
 - When the FOD check runs ("Checking if feature-on-demand installation would help"), it **re-runs the entire version computation** ÔÇö producing a second `Input values for ComputeVersionString` block. This is the shim asking "if .NET 3.5 were installed, would that resolve this request?"
 - A `GetRequestedRuntime` call may internally trigger `GetFileVersion` and config parsing
 
 The key to reading these is: watch for the major entry point lines (`FunctionCall:`, `MethodCall:`) to distinguish top-level activations from internal re-entrant calls.
 
 ## .NET 3.5 / v2.0.50727 Version Mapping
 
 In the logs, all version strings refer to the **CLR runtime version**, not the .NET Framework marketing version:
 
 | Runtime Version String | CLR Version | .NET Framework Versions |
 |----------------------|-------------|------------------------|
 | `v1.0.3705` | CLR 1.0 | .NET Framework 1.0 |
 | `v1.1.4322` | CLR 1.1 | .NET Framework 1.1 |
 | `v2.0.50727` | CLR 2.0 | .NET Framework 2.0, 3.0, 3.5 |
 | `v4.0.30319` | CLR 4.0 | .NET Framework 4.0 through 4.8.x |
 
 .NET 2.0, 3.0, and 3.5 all share CLR v2.0 ÔÇö the "3.0" and "3.5" releases added libraries on top of the same runtime. When the shim resolves to `v2.0.50727` or FOD offers to install "NetFx3", it's all about CLR v2.0.
 
 ## Shim HRESULT Codes
 
 These HRESULTs are returned by the shim to callers. They don't appear in the activation logs themselves (which use human-readable messages), but knowing them helps connect caller-side errors to activation log analysis:
 
 | HRESULT | Symbol | Meaning |
 |---------|--------|---------|
 | `0x80131700` | `CLR_E_SHIM_RUNTIMELOAD` | Cannot find or load a suitable runtime. The most common shim error ÔÇö corresponds to "ERROR: Unable to find a version of the runtime to use" in logs. |
 | `0x80131701` | `CLR_E_SHIM_RUNTIMEEXPORT` | Found a runtime but failed to get a required export or interface. |
 | `0x80131702` | `CLR_E_SHIM_INSTALLROOT` | .NET Framework install root missing or invalid in registry. |
 | `0x80131703` | `CLR_E_SHIM_INSTALLCOMP` | A required installation component is missing. |
 | `0x80131704` | `CLR_E_SHIM_LEGACYRUNTIMEALREADYBOUND` | A different runtime is already bound as the legacy runtime ÔÇö a legacy API tried to bind to a conflicting version. |
 | `0x80131705` | `CLR_E_SHIM_SHUTDOWNINPROGRESS` | The shim is shutting down. |

3. ## How COM Objects End Up in mscoree.dll
4. 
5. When a COM object is implemented in managed code (or is a CLR-internal component like diasymreader), its `InprocServer32` registry entry points to `mscoree.dll`. When `CoCreateInstance` is called for such a CLSID, the OS loads mscoree.dll and calls its `DllGetClassObject`.
6. 
7. The shim then must determine:
8. 1. Which CLR version should host this COM object
9. 2. Whether the CLR is already loaded in this process

12. ## CLSID Registry Layout
13. 
14. ```
15. HKCR\CLSID\{guid}
16. Ôöé   (Default) = "Friendly Name"
17. Ôöé   MasterCLSID = "{other-guid}"    (optional, for redirected CLSIDs)

21.     Ôöé   ThreadingModel = "Both"
22.     Ôöé
23.     ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ 2.0.50727                    (version subkey ÔÇö CLR v2 registration)
24.     Ôöé   Ôöé   (Default) = "2.0.50727"
25.     Ôöé   Ôöé   Assembly = "FullAssemblyName"       (managed interop)
26.     Ôöé   Ôöé   Class = "Namespace.ClassName"       (managed interop)

28.     Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ImplementedInThisVersion = ""       (native CLR component, not interop)
29.     Ôöé
30.     ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ 4.0.30319                    (version subkey ÔÇö CLR v4 registration)
31.         Ôöé   (Default) = "4.0.30319"

34.         Ôöé   RuntimeVersion = "v4.0.30319"
35.         ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ImplementedInThisVersion = ""

38. ### Key Registry Values
39. 
40. **`(Default)` under InprocServer32**: Must be `mscoree.dll` (or full path) for the shim to handle it.
41. 
42. **Version subkeys** (e.g., `2.0.50727`, `4.0.30319`): Each subkey represents a CLR version that registered this CLSID. The shim uses these to determine which runtime version(s) can host the object.
43. 
44. **`RuntimeVersion`**: The CLR version to load for this object. Read from the highest applicable version subkey.
45. 
46. **`Assembly` and `Class`**: For managed COM interop ÔÇö the managed assembly and type that implement the COM object. If present, the CLR loads this assembly and creates the managed type.
47. 
48. **`ImplementedInThisVersion`**: When this value exists (even if empty), it marks the COM class as a **native CLR component** ÔÇö something implemented inside the runtime itself (like the metadata APIs, symbol readers, etc.), not a managed interop object. The shim handles these differently from managed COM.
49. 
50. **`SupportedRuntimeVersions`** (directly under InprocServer32): Semicolon-delimited list of runtime versions. Acts like a `<supportedRuntime>` list in a config file. Example: `v2.0.50727;v4.0.30319`.
51. 
52. **`MasterCLSID`**: If present on the CLSID key, redirects to another CLSID for version lookup purposes. Used for COM objects that have been superseded.
53. 
54. ## The DllGetClassObject Decision Flow
55. 
56. ```
57. DllGetClassObject(rclsid, riid, ppv)
58. Ôöé
59. ÔöťÔöÇ 1. Is this an AppX process with a disallowed CLSID? Ôćĺ E_NOTIMPL

90. ## Why Native Build Tools Trigger COM Activation
91. 
92. Native tools like `link.exe`, `mt.exe`, `CL.exe` are not .NET applications, but they may use COM objects that are implemented inside the CLR:
93. 
94. - **Diasymreader** (`{E5CB7A31-7512-11D2-89CE-0080C792E5D8}`): Debug symbol writer/reader, used by tools that produce or consume PDB files
95. - **SymBinder** (`{0A29FF9E-7F9C-4437-8B11-F424491E3931}`): Symbol binder for debug information
96. - **ALink** (`{B79B0ACD-F5CD-409B-B5A5-A16244610B92}`): Assembly linker
97. 
98. These COM objects have `InprocServer32 = mscoree.dll` and go through the full shim activation path. Because they're activated via `DllGetClassObject` (not via a managed EXE launch), they take the **legacy COM path** with `IsCapped: 1`.
99. 
100. ## The Legacy Runtime Binding Order Problem
101. 
102. The order of activations within a process determines behavior:
103. 
104. ### Scenario A: Primary load first, COM activation second (common for link.exe, CL.exe)
105. 
106. ```
107. 1. ClrCreateInstance Ôćĺ ICLRMetaHostPolicy::GetRequestedRuntime
108.    Ôćĺ Config says supportedRuntime v4.0 Ôćĺ Loads v4.0 Ôćĺ Success
109.    Ôćĺ g_pLegacyAPIRuntimeInfo may or may not be set yet
110. 
111. 2. DllGetClassObject for diasymreader
112.    Ôćĺ Legacy bind, IsCapped: 1
113.    Ôćĺ If g_pLegacyAPIRuntimeInfo is set to v4.0 Ôćĺ uses v4.0 Ôćĺ OK
114.    Ôćĺ If NOT set Ôćĺ FindLatestVersion (capped to v2.0) Ôćĺ fails on machines without 3.5
115. ```
116. 
117. ### Scenario B: COM activation is the ONLY activation (common for mt.exe)
118. 
119. ```
120. 1. DllGetClassObject for diasymreader
121.    Ôćĺ Legacy bind, IsCapped: 1
122.    Ôćĺ No legacy runtime bound yet
123.    Ôćĺ No config file
124.    Ôćĺ FindLatestVersion (capped to v2.0) Ôćĺ (null) Ôćĺ ERROR Ôćĺ FOD
125. ```
126. 
127. ### Scenario C: Legacy hosting API sets the runtime first
128. 
129. ```
130. 1. CorBindToRuntimeEx(v4.0) Ôćĺ binds g_pLegacyAPIRuntimeInfo to v4.0
131. 2. DllGetClassObject for anything Ôćĺ legacy bind Ôćĺ uses g_pLegacyAPIRuntimeInfo (v4.0) Ôćĺ OK
132. ```
133. 
134. ## Diagnosing CLSID Issues
135. 
136. ### Check the CLSID registration
137. 
138. ```powershell
139. $clsid = '{E5CB7A31-7512-11D2-89CE-0080C792E5D8}'
140. 
141. # Main CLSID entry
142. Get-ItemProperty "Registry::HKCR\CLSID\$clsid" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
143. 
144. # InprocServer32 (should be mscoree.dll)
145. Get-ItemProperty "Registry::HKCR\CLSID\$clsid\InprocServer32" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
146. 
147. # Version subkeys ÔÇö what runtimes registered this CLSID?
148. Get-ChildItem "Registry::HKCR\CLSID\$clsid\InprocServer32" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
149.     ForEach-Object {
150.         Write-Output "`n--- $($_.PSChildName) ---"
151.         Get-ItemProperty "Registry::$($_.Name)" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
152.     }
153. ```
154. 
155. ### What to look for
156. 
157. | Observation | Meaning |
158. |-------------|---------|
159. | Only a `4.0.30319` subkey exists, no `2.0.50727` | .NET 3.5 was never installed (no v2 registration). Capped legacy binds will fail. |
160. | Both `2.0.50727` and `4.0.30319` subkeys exist | Both runtimes have registered this CLSID. Capped binds can use v2, uncapped can use v4. |
161. | `ImplementedInThisVersion` is present | This is a native CLR component (not managed interop). |
162. | `Assembly` and `Class` are present | This is a managed COM interop registration. |
163. | `SupportedRuntimeVersions` exists | Acts as an inline supportedRuntime list (semicolon-delimited). |
164. | `InprocServer32\(Default)` is NOT mscoree.dll | This CLSID is not CLR-hosted ÔÇö shim is not involved. |
165. 
166. ### Registration corruption from build races
167. 
168. If a build process registers/unregisters COM objects (e.g., via `regasm.exe`), concurrent registrations can corrupt CLSID entries:
169. 
170. - A version subkey may be deleted or partially written
171. - `Assembly`/`Class` values may be missing or point to the wrong type
172. - `RuntimeVersion` may be incorrect
173. 
174. Check for partial registrations by verifying that all expected values exist under each version subkey.

`ClrCreateInstance` Ôćĺ `GetRequestedRuntime` Ôćĺ reads `link.exe.config` Ôćĺ picks `v4.0.30319` Ôćĺ **success**. This uses the **modern metahost path** (`IsLegacyBind: 0`, `IsCapped: 0`).

### ÔŁî Activation 2 ÔÇö COM activation for SymBinder (fails)
`DllGetClassObject` for CLSID `{0A29FF9E-7F9C-4437-8B11-F424491E3931}` (NDP SymBinder, used for debug symbol binding). This takes the **legacy COM path** (`IsLegacyBind: 1`, `IsCapped: 1`).

Despite the config listing `v4.0.30319`, `v4.5`, and `v4.7` as supported runtimes, the log shows:
```
V2.0 Capping is preventing consideration of a newer runtime   ÔćÉ repeated 3x (once per entry)
Default version of the runtime on the machine: (null)
ERROR: Unable to find a version of the runtime to use.
```

The capped legacy bind **cannot see v4.0+**, and .NET 3.5 (v2.0.50727) is not installed, so it finds **nothing**.

## Why It's Intermittent

This is a **race condition on legacy runtime binding order**. The key field in the config is:

```
UseLegacyV2RuntimeActivationPolicy is set to 0
```

Because this is `0`, loading v4.0 through the metahost path (Activation 1) does **not** set `g_pLegacyAPIRuntimeInfo` ÔÇö the process-global legacy runtime pointer. Whether the SymBinder COM activation succeeds depends on whether something else sets the legacy runtime to v4.0 *before* `DllGetClassObject` runs:

| Run | What happens first | SymBinder result |
|-----|--------------------|------------------|
| Lucky | Some other legacy API call binds v4.0 as the legacy runtime | Ôťů Reuses v4.0 |
| Unlucky (this log) | SymBinder COM activation runs first, no legacy runtime bound yet | ÔŁî Capped search finds nothing |

Additionally, `SEM_FAILCRITICALERRORS` is `1` (inherited from the parent build process), so the failure is **silent** ÔÇö no FOD dialog, just a returned `CLR_E_SHIM_RUNTIMELOAD` (0x80131700). The intermittent nature makes it look like a flaky build.

## Fix

Set `useLegacyV2RuntimeActivationPolicy="true"` in `link.exe.config`:

```xml
<configuration>
  <startup useLegacyV2RuntimeActivationPolicy="true">
    <supportedRuntime version="v4.0.30319"/>
    <supportedRuntime version="v4.5"/>
    <supportedRuntime version="v4.7"/>
  </startup>
</configuration>
```

This lifts the Whidbey cap for legacy codepaths, letting the capped COM activation see v4.0 and succeed **deterministically** ÔÇö eliminating the race. The trade-off (locking out pre-v4 runtimes from the process) is fine for a build tool like `link.exe`.
